title: Vietnam & Bali: From Ancient Temples to Tropical Paradises

days.day: Sunday, 22.02.2026 - Da Nang arrival and chill

checkpoints.title: Transfer to Da Nang
checkpoints.description: Take car from Hoi An to Da Nang hotel.

checkpoints.title: Hotel Check-in & Relax
checkpoints.description: Settle into the hotel and unwind. Check out the infinity pool and beach access.

checkpoints.title: Ăn hải sản
checkpoints.description: Either at Mộc Quán or Bé Mặn or Năm Đảnh

checkpoints.title: Dragon Bridge Show
checkpoints.description: Catch the fire and water show at the Dragon Bridge

checkpoints.title: Son Tra night market
checkpoints.description: Explore the night market for local snacks and souvenirs.

checkpoints.title: Cocktail at a United Bar
checkpoints.description: Enjoy a nightcap at this popular bar.

checkpoints.title: Traveling to Linh Ứng Pagoda
checkpoints.description: Car picks us up at 8AM. Drive is quite fast, can take a view.

checkpoints.title: Linh Ứng Pagoda
checkpoints.description: There are actually three Linh Ung Pagodas in Da Nang, but the one on the Son Tra Peninsula (at Bai But) is the largest, newest, and most famous. Back in the 19th century under Emperor Minh Mang, a Buddhist statue miraculously drifted ashore here. The local fishermen built a small shrine to honor it. Legend says that from that day forward, the seas calmed, typhoons bypassed the city, and fishing prospered. The absolute centerpiece is the 67-meter-tall (about 30 stories high!) white statue of Avalokitesvara, the Goddess of Mercy. It is the tallest Buddha statue in Vietnam. She faces the ocean to watch over and protect the local fishermen. It took six full years (2004-2010) to build, largely because of the immense scale of the project. The statue was crafted by highly skilled stone artisans from the famous Non Nuoc Marble Village (which sits right at the base of the Marble Mountains we'll be visiting later that afternoon). Some cool details: The statue actually has 17 interior floors inside of it, each housing dozens of smaller Buddha statues! Also, leading up to the main temple are 18 intricate statues of Arhats, each depicting a different human emotion (joy, anger, love, hate, etc.). A quick heads-up: You are visiting a very active religious site, so make sure your shoulders and knees are covered when you go!

checkpoints.title: đền thờ Mẫu
checkpoints.description: Đền thờ Mẫu are dedicated to Đạo Mẫu (Vietnamese Mother Goddess worship), which was officially recognized by UNESCO in 2016 as Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity. This religion is uniquely Vietnamese and blends folk beliefs, Taoism, and Buddhism. Unlike many religions centered on male gods, Đạo Mẫu focuses on female divine figures who govern different realms: Mẫu Thượng Thiên – Heaven | Mẫu Thượng Ngàn – Mountains & Forests | Mẫu Thoải – Water | Mẫu Địa – Earth. These goddesses are believed to protect families, bring prosperity, and control natural elements. While Linh Ứng Pagoda attracts large tourist crowds, Đền thờ Mẫu tends to be less commercial and more locally focused, offering a more intimate spiritual experience.

checkpoints.title: Transfer to Intercontinental Danang Sun Peninsula Resort
checkpoints.description: Pay attention to the monkeys. Tell driver to pull over and take pics.

checkpoints.title: Intercontinental Danang Sun Peninsula Resort
checkpoints.description: This is the most luxurious hotel in Da Nang, perched on a private bay with stunning views. It was designed by the famous architect Bill Bensley and has won multiple awards for its unique blend of traditional Vietnamese design and modern luxury. The restaurants are sorted into themes and yep, it is very expensive, not for us!

checkpoints.title: Drinks at The Long Bar
checkpoints.description: The bar’s name literally reflects its impressive length — about 50 meters of seating and social space along the beachfront.

checkpoints.title: Bàn Cờ Peak
checkpoints.description:
Highest point in Da Nang known as Roof of Da Nang. Name translates to Chessboard Peak. According to local folklore, an ancient fairy came down from the heavens, was captivated by the view, and set up a stone chessboard. Another deity named De Thich (a master chess player) passed by and challenged him. They played for days to a standstill. Suddenly, De Thich was distracted by the laughter of beautiful fairies bathing at the nearby beach (now named Tien Sa beach). He made a careless move and lost. The winner flew back to heaven, leaving De Thich alone to ponder the board. 
Right at the summit, there is a bronze statue of De Thich staring intensely at a stone chessboard. It is a massive tourist tradition to sit in the empty seat across from him, pretend to ponder a move, and take a photo!

checkpoints.title: Lunch at Madame Lân
checkpoints.description: Featured in the Michelin Guide (Michelin Selected). Named in honor of the owner’s late mother. Fried fish summer roll – a Da Nẵng specialty that combines crispy texture with fresh herbs and dipping sauce. Mì Quảng – a quintessential Central Vietnamese turmeric noodle dish with savory broth, herbs, and toppings. Bún bò Huế / Bún bò – rich beef noodle soup from Central Vietnam.

checkpoints.title: Marble Mountains Caves
checkpoints.description: Climbing limestone peaks to explore massive, naturally formed caves hidden in the forest.

checkpoints.title: Beach Sunset
checkpoints.description: Escaping the city crowds for a quiet, peaceful sunset swim on pristine white sand.

checkpoints.title: Transfer to Dalat stay
checkpoints.description: 37 Trần Văn Côi, F5 | Taxi should cost around 150-200k according to the homestay's owner. Ask driver to stop at a place to get takeaway dinner.

checkpoints.title: Arrival at Homestay & Dinner
checkpoints.description: Check-in and enjoy a cozy dinner at the homestay.

checkpoints.title: Lululola Music Show
checkpoints.description: Without reservation. Pay on place. Just live music and some drinks.

checkpoints.title: Chợ Đà Lạt (optional)
checkpoints.description: If not too tired, take drive (15 min) to the night market for a quick stroll and maybe some late-night snacks.

checkpoints.title: Local Coffee Plantation Visit
checkpoints.description: Learning about Da Lat’s famous coffee culture.

days.day: Thursday, 26.02.2026 - Heading to the Big City

checkpoints.title: Transfer to Ho Chi Minh City
checkpoints.description: Flight or bus ride down from the highlands to Saigon.

checkpoints.title: Saigon Nightlife
checkpoints.description: Exploring District 1 or a rooftop bar.

checkpoints.title: War Remnants Museum / Independence Palace
checkpoints.description: Learning about Vietnam's modern history.

checkpoints.title: Ben Thanh Market
checkpoints.description: Shopping and street food exploration.

checkpoints.title: Transfer to Tan Son Nhat Airport
checkpoints.description: Flight from HCM to Denpasar (Bali).

checkpoints.title: Bali Arrival & Uluwatu
checkpoints.description: Check-in and sunset views in Southern Bali.

checkpoints.title: Tegalalang Rice Terraces
checkpoints.description: Iconic Balinese rice fields and swings.

checkpoints.title: Nusa Penida or Beach Club
checkpoints.description: Optional day trip to the islands or relaxing at a beach club.

checkpoints.title: Final Souvenirs & Departure
checkpoints.description: Wrapping up the trip and heading to the airport.
